# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-deployment
  namespace: development
  labels:
    app: my-application
    environment: development
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-application
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-application
        environment: development
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 10000
        runAsGroup: 10000
        fsGroup: 10000
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: dockerhub-credentials
      containers:
        - name: my-application
          # Pin to a specific digest rather than using :latest tag.
          # Using :latest is insecure because it can pull unexpected or
          # tampered images without notice, breaking reproducibility and
          # enabling supply-chain attacks. Always pin to an immutable
          # digest and update it deliberately through your CI/CD pipeline.
          image: myorg/my-application@sha256:abc123def456789012345678901234567890123456789012345678901234abcd
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
              protocol: TCP
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            runAsNonRoot: true
            runAsUser: 10000
            runAsGroup: 10000
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "128Mi"
              cpu: "100m"
            limits:
              memory: "256Mi"
              cpu: "500m"
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 5
          env:
            - name: APP_ENV
              value: "development"
            # Secrets are referenced from Kubernetes Secrets, never hardcoded
            - name: DB_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: app-secrets
                  key: db-password
          volumeMounts:
            - name: tmp-volume
              mountPath: /tmp
      volumes:
        - name: tmp-volume
          emptyDir:
            sizeLimit: "100Mi"
